what_it_does: Stores a JSON array of extracted color-palette observations and generator rules from multiple pitch-deck and landing-page sources, each entry naming a palette token or pattern, describing where it appears, and prescribing how to use it.
observations: Each finding has category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent flag, generator_rule, _src, and _depth fields.
The dominant accent is a warm gold/amber with many sampled hex variants across sources, e.g. #C8A800, #C8A84B, #B8960C, #C9A227, #C8960A, #E0A82C, #b8952a.
Near-black warm text (#1A1A1A / #1E1E1A / #222220) and warm cream backgrounds (#F8F6F2 / #FAFAF8 / #F7F5F0) are consistently documented.

Rare colors are treated as semantic signals: maroon for entry/sustained commitment, green-teal for transaction nodes, orange-amber for financial outcomes, green/red for directional market indicators, red for negative comparison marks.
Some findings are marked emergent=true, such as rare-color semantics, two-tier gold, chart-palette gradients, olive-gold label variant, and hatched-cream texture zones.
Multiple sources are represented: capital-raise, company-info, deck1-2026, pitch-deck, presentation-15p, product-vi-landing, product-vt, recent-pitches.
The file is truncated mid-entry at the end.
standouts: The 'rare colors are semantic not decorative' finding frames scarcity itself as the signal.
A two-tier gold system is repeatedly observed: a brighter data/figure gold versus a darker title/section gold.
Green and red are reserved strictly for directional/semantic indicators, not general success/error states.
Chart palettes stay within the warm spectrum (gold, terracotta, warm grey) with no cool blues or greens.
Competitor brand blues are allowed to exist as-is in comparison diagrams, standing in contrast to the warm gold system.
A hatched-cream texture zone is used as a footer/data band signal across several decks.
conventions: Findings follow a consistent schema with recipe, evidence, generator_rule, and source metadata.
Each generator_rule is prescriptive and often uses absolute language ('never', 'only', 'exclusively').
Color tokens are described both by approximate hex and by semantic role.
Emergent findings are explicitly flagged and tend to describe exceptions or higher-order patterns.
concerns: The same conceptual token (brand gold) has many slightly different hex values across sources, which could complicate canonical token reconciliation.
The file is truncated, so the last entry is incomplete.
Some findings overlap heavily (e.g. multiple 'primary gold' entries) without explicit cross-source merging or conflict annotation.
A few rules appear source-specific but are stated as global ('never use white as a background'), which may need context-aware interpretation.
notes: A ledger of per-source color palette observations and emergent generator rules extracted from pitch decks and landing pages.
questions: Are the hex variations measurement noise, compression artifacts, or intentional source-specific variants?
How should overlapping gold tokens from different sources be reconciled into a single canonical palette?
Are rare semantic colors (maroon, green-teal, orange-amber) global rules or tied only to the capital-raise source?
What is missing after the truncation point?
purpose_vs_actual: The file acts as a findings ledger for palette tokens and usage rules, which matches its apparent purpose.
apparent_themes: Warm, premium, print-like palette with gold as the sole chromatic accent.
Semantic scarcity: rare colors mean specific things because they are rare.
Warm neutrals only: no pure black, no pure white, no cool greys or blues in the core system.
Consistency across compression depths: same language expressed in 1-page, 15-page, 30-page, and landing-page formats.
Isolated semantic overrides: green/red and competitor blues are allowed only in narrow, well-defined contexts.
intention_signals: To encode a unified, warm, scarcity-driven brand color language and provide prescriptive generator rules so future collateral can reproduce the same palette across formats and compression levels.
novelty: The interpretive framing that color rarity itself carries semantic weight, and the systematic extraction of palette rules across multiple source depths with emergent/exception annotations.
contribution: Preserves per-source palette observations, sampled hex values, and emergent generator rules that a fused system can reconcile into canonical tokens and context-specific exceptions.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON ledger of color palette findings extracted from multiple pitch-deck and landing-page sources, documenting gold-accent systems, warm neutrals, rare semantic colors, and generator rules.
intention_for_embedding: To capture the brand's color language and rules so future design generation can reproduce the warm, premium, scarcity-driven palette across formats.
